nodes.context: Chinese-American criminal organization/gang formed as a labor organization in New York City's Chinatown, involved in drug trafficking and vice.
Rival tong to Hip Sing, involved in Tong wars for control of Chinatown in early 1900s.
Rival tong to Hip Sing, involved in Tong wars for control of Chinatown in early 1900s.
Chinese political party that collaborated with Hip Sing in drug trafficking during 1930s-1940s.
Street gang used by Hip Sing Tong to carry out crimes in New York City.

edges.context: Hip Sing and On Leong were rivals in Tong wars for control of Chinatown in early 1900s.
Hip Sing and Four Brothers were rivals in Tong wars for control of Chinatown in early 1900s.
Hip Sing collaborated with Kuomintang in drug trafficking operations during 1930s-1940s.
Hip Sing Tong secretly used Flying Dragons to carry out crimes, but control frayed by 1994.
